title: The Underworld Stirs
description: On pages 1–3, this part follows Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Jarul, Gerd, Chopper, Unknown person #3 as Elbaf’s school, underworld, and giant society fall into crisis as children, giants, and the Straw Hats react to dangerous forces moving beneath the island.
scene: Elbaf’s school, underworld, and giant society fall into crisis as children, giants, and the Straw Hats react to dangerous forces moving beneath the island.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Wow! My legs made me veer off!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Where are you going?! You're going to fall!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But it's fine! Don't worry about me, YLVA!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Come back, ro- nya! its danger- ous!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: The first brat is about to "fall."

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: You will fall to the under- world!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Perfect! Make sure all of Elbaf hears her voice!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Alright! If in 30 seconds you still haven't made a decision... I'll drop the first kid!

dialogue.speaker: Jarul
dialogue.text: JARUL OF THE BEARDED MOUN- TAIN!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: to set fire to the school and the li- brary!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This library holds texts passed down from past generations for Miller- NIA!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But their lives come first! Do you even realize what you're saying?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The problem isn't their demands! It's the decision we have to make...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Whether to submit to these guys or not!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Are you suggest- ing we just let our kids die?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Isn't there anyone nearby?! We must put an end to this absurd game at all costs!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's too risky to face them before we orders- tend their powers.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: But I can't control my emotions anymore!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even the world's worst criminals don't take children as hostages!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: GIhahaha! Cut the sob story, it doesn't affect me!

summary: This part advances the Elbaf Crisis through 41 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on the immediate conflict, character reactions, and the larger Elbaf mystery.
